_meta.description: SERA Vault OS - shared project memory. Read this at the start of every session. Update it after completing work.

definition.name: SERA Vault OS
definition.purpose: A local-first Research-as-a-Service operating system integrated with Obsidian. Helps create client research vaults, generate research questions and hypotheses, run experiments, log results, select winning experiments, and generate client-ready research reports.

session_1.label: CLI
integration_notes: Engine stubs expect: from engine.hypothesis import generate(client, brief_id)
Engine stubs expect: from engine.experiment import create(client, hypothesis_id)
Reports stub expects: from reports.generator import generate(client, brief_id)
All output uses rich tables and ASCII-safe text (no em dashes, no Unicode symbols)

session_3.label: Experiment Engine

public_functions.engine.results.log_result: log_result(client, experiment_id, condition, metric, value, notes) -> str

integration_notes: create() reads from hypotheses/, writes to experiments/exp-NNN.md with status=pending
select_winner() marks winner=true in result frontmatter and status=complete on experiment
Anthropic API (claude-sonnet-4-6) used if ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var is set; fallback always works
All result files store condition, metric, value in frontmatter for winner comparison
list_results() returns list of dicts with all frontmatter fields + path key
winner dict keys: winner_id, winner_condition, winner_metric, winner_value, experiment_id, total_results

session_4.label: Report Generator
session_4.description: Generates client-ready research reports from experiment results.
engine_contract: Use engine.results.list_results(client, experiment_id) to read all results
Use engine.winner.select_winner result dict for winner summary in reports
All result frontmatter: id, experiment_id, client_id, condition, metric, value, winner, confidence, status, recorded_on

rules.session_ownership: Each session owns only its assigned folder. Never modify another session folder.
rules.read_nemp_first: Every session must read nemp_memory.json before starting any work.
rules.update_nemp_after: Every session must update nemp_memory.json after completing work.
rules.shared_imports: Sessions may import from shared/ but not from each other folders.
rules.git_worktrees: Parallel sessions use git worktrees. Each worktree = one session = one branch.
rules.no_overwrite: Never overwrite existing work. Read first, then decide.

next_steps: Session 4 (reports/): Build Jinja2-based report generator - expose reports.generator.generate(client, brief_id)
Session 4 reads engine results via engine.results.list_results() and engine.winner summary dicts
Merge all session branches into main when all 4 sessions are complete

worktree_setup.note: Run from the main sera-vault-os directory. Creates a sibling folder per session.
